code_path_proof.model_bridge: core/model_bridge.py approved intent builder now includes the richer payload keys in the intent dict.
code_path_proof.bot_core: bot_core.py bridge branch still reads only intent['slug'], intent['side'], intent['confidence'], intent['edge'], and intent['entry_px'] to construct a Signal and open a trade.

answers.tp_sl_size_fields_preserved_when_present: Yes in the approved intent object, but not yet consumed by bot_core execution handling.
answers.freshness_fields_preserved: Yes in the approved intent object, but not yet used downstream by bot_core execution logic.
answers.reasons_matchup_held_outcome_preserved: Yes in the approved intent object, but not yet consumed by bot_core execution handling.
answers.silent_drop_status: The richer payload is not dropped inside model_bridge anymore, but much of it is still dropped at the bot_core practical-use boundary because bot_core ignores it.

conclusion: The richer bridge payload is clearly present and preserved in the approved intent object, but end-to-end execution-facing use is only partial because bot_core still consumes a thin subset. Therefore the bridge contract is PARTIAL, not fully VERIFIED.
